behavior_summary: Provides the 'U8P_TOSSROCK::look' (examine) behavior for a tossable rock object: when the rock is examined, the script prepares a short literal string "rock " and invokes the item's description/bark routine so the engine displays that text to the player or the requesting process.

Before the bark call the handler sets object/info-related flags and a process-exclude flag — likely to control who receives the message or to avoid re-entering the caller — then passes a PID/context and the temporary string to Item::bark.
After the bark call the temporary string is freed, the return value is inspected by an implies/pop sequence (appears to force a truthy/handled return), and the handler suspends/returns.
notable_control_flow: Entry on the 'U8P_TOSSROCK::look' trigger leads to two engine flag writes (set info and process_exclude) that adjust call context.
Allocates a short literal string "rock ", pushes a PID/context and the object pointer, then calls external Item::bark.
Frees the temporary string after the call, checks the bark return value using an implies/pop idiom, then suspends/returns.
open_questions: What exact info key/value is being written by the 'set info' opcode (disassembly shows an info write but the concrete info id is unclear) — this is inferred but not certain.
Which PID is pushed as the call context (the viewer's process, the rock's process, or another actor) — the code pushes a pid but the precise source is not explicit.
What precisely does the 'implies 01 01' / pop sequence enforce semantically in the engine (appears to convert/ensure a handled/true return but the exact effect is unclear).
Why is process_exclude set around this call — to prevent feedback to the caller, to avoid re-entrancy, or for another engine-specific reason?

main_function.name: U8P_TOSSROCK::look
main_function.purpose: Handle the 'U8P_TOSSROCK::look' verb for the tossable rock: set up context flags, produce a short descriptive message "rock ", invoke the item's bark routine to display that description, clean up temporary resources, and return.
entry_conditions: Event 0 (U8P_TOSSROCK::look) — triggered when a player/actor examines or 'looks' at the tossable rock object

function_map.name: U8P_TOSSROCK::look
function_map.role: U8P_TOSSROCK::look handler that emits the rock's short description

function_map.name: Item::bark
function_map.role: Engine routine used to present or append a short text message coming from an item/process (used here to show the rock's description)

external_calls.purpose: Pass a context PID and the literal string "rock " so the item/process system will present the rock's short description to the player/process.

writes.name: info (object info/state)
writes.likely_effect: Sets per-object info/state used to influence the bark/display or object metadata (exact info id/value inferred from opcode usage).

writes.name: process_exclude
writes.likely_effect: Marks a process or context to be excluded from some processing (likely to avoid sending the bark back to the originator or to prevent re-entrancy).
